old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because broad charcoal production is controlled wood carbonization under restricted air; pottery vessels and ceramic kilns are not required for earth-covered or closed-space carbonization.

source_shape.source_locator: Section 1.4.1 defines charcoal as wood carbonized under controlled conditions in a closed space or kiln by restricting air entry so wood forms charcoal rather than ash.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source defines charcoal production by controlled air restriction and pyrolysis, not by pottery vessels or ceramic firing.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Pottery remains relevant to separate ceramic and smelting nodes, but the charcoal-production source supports earth/closed-space carbonization as the process boundary.

ontology_before: Charcoal Production depended directly on Pottery, implying fired-clay vessels or ceramic kiln practice enabled basic charcoal production.
ontology_after: Charcoal Production depends on fire control and is scoped to controlled wood carbonization; pottery remains separate ceramic infrastructure and a direct smelting prerequisite where needed.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pottery is no longer a direct prerequisite for charcoal_production.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to ceramic-retort charcoal production rather than broad wood carbonization.
A stronger source showed earliest intentional charcoal production required pottery vessels or ceramic kilns.
The pottery node were broadened to represent all closed-space pyrotechnology rather than fired clay vessels.
short_reason: Basic charcoal production needs controlled carbonization, not pottery.
